calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Terminating The Eliana Yoneda Trust
Off Calendar May-23-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stuart List for The Northern Trust Company. Helaina Titus (Trust Officer), Jane Swan (Portfolio Manager) and Roger Kummert (Real Estate Manager) from The Northern Trust Company also present.

Mr. List provided a description of the real property in Mendocino County, explained what repairs were made and which repairs still need to be completed (interior of the home). Mr. Kummert provided a more detailed discussion as to what repairs were done to which portions of the home. Ms. Titus reported that there is no cash in the trust after the contractors and attorney's fees were paid, and that Northern Trust's fee is discounted due to the on going relationships. The parties also reported that it would take about an additional $20,000.00 to complete repairs, spoke of change of circumstances, stated that there intent was to preserve and protect the property for the beneficiary, and assured the Court that Eliana is a mature responsible young lady. The Petition for Order Terminating the Eliana Toneda Trust was taken under submission.

Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Nancy Bautista/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Petition For Order Terminating The Eliana Yoneda Trust
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Continued To May-20-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Terminating The Eliana Yoneda Trust
Off Calendar Mar-18-2013 Continued To Apr-15-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: L. Stuart List for The Northern Trust Company (Petitioner).

The Court pointed out that a tremendous amount of money has been spent for renovations of the property and that a tremendous amount is still needed to complete the renovations. Counsel explained that the property is over 100 years old, that it has not been improved and that the house was in horrible condition. Counsel also stated that he does not have information regarding the initial bid for the cost of renovations, that the Co-Trustees and the family were involved in the decision to renovate the property, that the beneficiary has sentimental value with the home and intends to see the renovations completed using her own funds. Furthermore, Mr. List stated that if the Trust is not terminated, then the small amount of funds will be depleated, that if they did nothing further that they would have to sell the property and that nobody in this case questioned whether the renovations were needed. The Petition Order Terminating Trust was continued to April 15, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing a decleration from the beneficiary as to what she wants to do with the property and a decleration from the Co-Trustee (father).

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Petition For Order Terminating The Eliana Yoneda Trust
Off Calendar Mar-04-2013 Continued To Mar-18-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
